behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff: reject path must both set HTTP 401 and prevent enqueue. MOCK-MISS: this is not just hash math; it is cross-module gating (auth decision -> side-effect suppression). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process HTTP harness + real handler wiring is enough. (b) no AI/ML output. (c) no multi-agentic chain. (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected higher level: System considered, but behavior text only requires header/body verification and 'not forwarded', not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with real signature-verification middleware and a spy/fake queue adapter wired as production. Send a request with invalid `X-Signature-256`; assert `401` and assert queue `enqueue` was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across modules (HTTP handler <-> persistent queue write). Unit is too low because a single-function test cannot expose misordered await/commit across components. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no explicit real network/OS dependency in behavior text; local persistent store wiring suffices. (b) no AI/ML output. (c) no multi-agentic sequence. (d) no LLM-stage handoff. Rejected higher level: System considered, but text does not require real HTTP transport or external queue service behavior; it requires write-before-ack ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process server + real queue implementation against local persistence (e.g., sqlite/test DB). Instrument timestamps/hooks around queue commit and response send; assert commit completes before `200` is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction: enqueue/dispatch should decouple response from processor completion. Unit test of one function can miss accidental await of downstream promise in wiring. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess strictly required; controlled in-process delayed processor reproduces bug. (b) no AI/ML output. (c) no multi-agentic chain. (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected higher level: System considered, but behavior text is about waiting semantics, which can be exposed with in-process delayed downstream dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real handler with downstream processor stub that blocks (e.g., unresolved promise for 10s). Send request and assert response returns quickly while processor remains unfinished; verify event still queued/processing started asynchronously.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA dominates: wall-clock SLA under concurrency is sensitive to real HTTP stack, event-loop scheduling, connection handling, and queue contention. MOCK-HIDE: fake timers/stubs can pass while real server misses latency target under load. Rejected lower Integration by targeted side-check: behavior explicitly requires concurrent slow earlier events and real-time bound ('within 3 seconds wall-clock'), which in-process fakes may not faithfully reproduce.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service process and issue concurrent HTTP requests while earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work. Measure end-to-end response latency at client side; assert p100 (or strict per-request) ack latency <= 3s under the defined concurrent scenario.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup is a state handoff/persistence concern (handler -> queue store uniqueness check). Unit-only logic test can miss duplicate insertion races or repository wiring mistakes. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess in wording; local persistent store test is sufficient baseline. (b) no AI/ML output. (c) no multi-agentic chain. (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected higher level: System considered, but behavior text does not require external queue service/network semantics, only dedup write behavior.
behaviors.test_description: With real queue persistence in test environment, submit same event ID twice through handler path; assert first write succeeds and second is ignored/rejected with no second queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is mapping of queue backpressure signal to HTTP contract across module boundary. Unit is too low because failure occurs when queue status propagates through handler response logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; a real/in-process queue component configured full can trigger path. (b) no AI/ML output. (c) no multi-agentic chain. (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected higher level: System considered, but wording is deterministic status translation ('queue reports full' -> `503` + header), not real network fault behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue to capacity reached (or pre-fill). Send request through in-process HTTP handler wiring; assert `503` and `Retry-After` present with expected value format.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component + persistence interaction (configuration, counting, reject-on-overflow). Unit with mocked store can hide real enforcement defects. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) behavior text does not explicitly require external OS/network/subprocess; real queue implementation against local persistence can catch true failure. (b) no AI/ML output. (c) no multi-agentic chain. (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected higher level: System considered, but no explicit external service/network requirement is stated; if actual deployment queue is remote, System becomes preferable, but that is not required by the given behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with small configured capacity (e.g., 2). Write up to limit, then attempt one more insert; assert overflow write is rejected and stored count remains at configured maximum.
